action: Camped west bank; fires burning; preparing to withdraw after midnight
Constructing earthworks on slightly elevated knoll east bank; NPS archaeology confirms earthworks exactly on original position; roughly 4ft high, 7ft wide, 2ft ditch outside
Flanking position east bank alongside Lillington; New Hanover Volunteer Company of Rangers; Caswell's report grouped Ashe's men with Lillington's, causing undercount in early sources
Camped approximately 6 miles northwest near modern Atkinson NC; MacDonald ill and relinquishing command to MacLeod; council of war deciding to attack at dawn

Withdrew across bridge to east bank after midnight; left campfires burning as deception; tore up bridge planks from beneath; smeared remaining log stringers with soap and animal fat; joined Lillington's position

Mother Covington (3-pounder) and daughter (half-pounder) positioned behind earthworks in direct line with bridge; additional 6 or 9-pounders per NPS park historians; several pieces loaded with swanshot — tin canisters spreading approximately 20 small bullets on impact
Column marching toward bridge at 1am; forming into two wings on approach road; Felix Kenan (ind_014) deserted Loyalist camp to warn Patriots of dawn attack
Advanced toward bridge; found Caswell's camp empty with fires still burning; believed Patriots in retreat; courier had earlier scouted Patriot position at midnight posing as flag-of-truce messenger

Saw empty Patriot camp with fires burning; fell back into woods to consult MacDonald; did NOT participate in charge; as AmRevNC notes — literally the right hand did not know what the left hand was doing; no communication possible between wings
Advance company of picked broadswordsmen reached west side of bridge; challenge exchange in Gaelic between Patriot sentry and Capt. Alexander McLean; sentry fired warning shot; MacLeod ordered charge

Charged across greased bridge stringers; forced to cross in single file on slippery logs; yelled 'King George and Broadswords'; Campbell killed at or near east bank; some fell into creek approximately 6ft deep

MacLeod led charge with Campbell; KIA on bridge. Source discrepancy on wound count: Moore AAR — 'upwards of twenty balls'; NCpedia — 9 bullets and 24 swan shot; Wikipedia — 20 musket balls. All accounts agree on death at bridge.

Held fire until Loyalists within approximately 30 paces; opened with muskets and artillery simultaneously; swanshot devastating to column forced into single file on bridge; battle lasted approximately 3 minutes
Fired at approximately 30 paces into column; swanshot from Mother Covington and daughter; additional pieces firing; devastating to Loyalists forced into single file crossing

Column collapsed and retreated northwest; MacDonald captured next day in his tent; approximately 850 total prisoners taken in following days including weapons, wagon train, and £15,000 sterling; Patriot casualties: 1 killed, 1 wounded
Patriots replaced bridge planks and crossed in pursuit of retreating Loyalists; Colonel Moore arrived on scene a few hours after the battle
One company led by one of Caswell's lieutenants forded creek above bridge to flank retreating Loyalists; officer unnamed in all sources consulted
